summary: Added repository-level line-ending policy via `.gitattributes` and normalized existing files to Unix-style line endings using `git add --renormalize .`. The PR was an infrastructure cleanup intended to prevent cross-platform CRLF/LF churn. The dump contains no diff or changed-file listing, so exact attributes and affected files are not visible in this slice.
what_changed: Created or updated `.gitattributes` to enforce Unix-style line endings for the repository.
Normalized existing tracked files with `git add --renormalize .`.
Based the line-ending configuration on GitHub documentation for per-repository line-ending settings.
smart_moves: Centralized line-ending behavior in `.gitattributes` instead of relying on each contributor's local Git configuration.
Performed a one-time renormalization so future diffs would not repeatedly include incidental CRLF/LF changes.
Kept the change focused on repository hygiene rather than mixing formatting policy with source changes.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation projects, stable line endings reduce noisy diffs and help reviewers focus on matching, naming, and structural changes.
Repository-wide normalization should be done as its own PR when possible, because it can touch many files and obscure semantic changes.
Use `.gitattributes` to encode text-file policy explicitly so contributors on Windows, macOS, and Linux produce consistent patches.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that introduced repository-wide formatting or normalization changes.
Find later PRs that modify `.gitattributes` or line-ending policy.
Check whether future large diffs were caused by renormalization or formatting-only changes.
